data.title: Singapore Projects Modest Trade Growth Amid Tariff Uncertainties — Update
data.description:
By Amanda Lee

Singapore's economy entered the new year in strong shape, notching its fastest pace of growth since 2021 as exports rebounded. But emerging risks are adding uncertainty to the trade-reliant financial hub's outlook.

The city-state's economy grew 4.4% last year, eclipsing the 1.8% expansion seen in 2023 and sealing its recovery from the slowdown sparked by the pandemic.

This year, that momentum is likely to slow, with the Ministry of Trade and Industry projecting growth at 1.0% to 3.0% as it flagged headwinds around global trade.

"Uncertainties in the global economy remain significant, with the risks tilted to the downside," Beh Swan Gin, permanent secretary at the ministry, said at a press briefing Friday.

Trade frictions among major economies and the potential escalation of geopolitical conflicts could drive up production costs and cause more global economic policy uncertainty, said the ministry. That would likely dampen global investment and trade, weighing growth, it added.

Slowdowns in Singapore's major trading partners would mean softer demand for its exports, with trade disruptions standing to deeply affect the small, open economy.

The ministry expects overall gross domestic product in key trading partners like China and the U.S. to ease this year from the pace seen in 2024.

"There is a large cone of uncertainty surrounding the outlook of the U.S. economy, with its trajectory depending on the policies of the new U.S. administration," it said.

Yong Yik Wei, chief economist at the ministry, warned of the indirect impact tariffs could have on Singapore.

Even though the city-state has not been directly targeted by tariffs, and likely faces little impact from U.S. tariffs for aluminum and steel, Yong alluded to Singapore's vulnerability.

"Obviously because we are small, we are open. Trade is three times our GDP, so when there are trade frictions, that would have an indirect impact on Singapore," she said.

Economists at Nomura expect weaker external demand under Trump 2.0 to weigh on Singapore's growth this year, but see scope for continued resilience. Wages and employment strength provide "some cushion against shocks from increased trade protectionism," Euben Paracuelles and Charnon Boonnuch wrote in a report.

Waning demand abroad could dampen Singapore's exports recovery after a return to growth last year, with outbound shipments rebounding in line with the global electronics upswing.

Non-oil domestic exports growth is likely to be modest this year, government agency Enterprise Singapore said Friday, noting significant trade risks.

"Uncertainties arising from ongoing trade frictions among major economies could result in a more challenging and competitive trade environment," the agency said.

It continues to project exports growth at 1.0% to 3.0% this year, which would still be better than last year's 0.2% expansion. Higher shipments of electronic products drove the 2024 result, reversing the 13.1% contraction seen in 2023, the data showed.

By destination, Hong Kong and Malaysia were the top markets for Singapore exports, while shipments to Japan contracted further.

Electronics exports, a key driver of the city-state's economic growth, rose 8.2% in 2024, bouncing back from 2023's 19.7% contraction.

DBS economist Chua Han Teng expects the economy to hold up in the first half of the year, noting that business sentiment remains positive among companies in manufacturing, wholesale trade, finance & insurance, and information & communications--sectors accounting for around 58% of nominal GDP.

Beyond that, he sees considerable downside risks from global trade policy shifts arising from Trump's tariff escalation.

"Singapore's economy remains indirectly vulnerable to potential U.S. tariffs on its major trading partners, and any resulting deceleration in global economic growth and trade," he said.

Write to Amanda Lee at amanda.lee@wsj.com

data.title: Hong Kong Shares Extend Gains Midday — Market Talk
data.description: Hong Kong shares extend gains at midday thanks to positive sentiment across several sectors on the adoption of DeepSeek's AI model in their services. Tech and healthcare stocks are leading the gains with Alibaba Health Information Technology up 16%, Wuxi Biologics adding 8.15% and WuXi AppTec rising 5.8%. JD.com and Kuaishou Technology are up 4.95% and 4.7%, respectively. Among the few decliners, SMIC drops 2.9% and WH Group falls 2.3%. The benchmark Hang Seng Index is up 2.2% at 22303.80 and the Hang Seng Tech Index gains 3.1%. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

data.title: Market Chatter: HD Korea Shipbuilding Unveils SMR-Powered Container Carrier Prototype
data.description:
HD Korea Shipbuilding & Offshore Engineering unveiled a prototype for a small modular reactor (SMR)-powered container carrier at the New Nuclear for Maritime Summit in Houston, US earlier this week, The Korea Herald reported Thursday.

Based on a 15,000 twenty-foot equivalent unit (TEU)-class vessel, the design received approval in principle from the American Bureau of Shipping, ensuring compliance with key safety standards, the report said.

The shipbuilder aims to finalize its marine nuclear business model by 2030, integrating enhanced safety features, including a double-tank radiation containment system, it said.

Shares of HD Korea Shipbuilding & Offshore Engineering fell nearly 6% in recent trade on Friday.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: India's Deepak Nitrite set for worst week in nearly five years
data.description:
** Shares of India's Deepak Nitrite fall ~10% to 2025 rupees

** Chemicals maker's Q3 consol net profit more than halved y/y to 980.9 million rupees ($11.3 million), rev from ops dropped over 5%

** Stock has lost 17% so far this week, set for its worst week since mid-March 2020

** Trading vols 1.7 mln shares vs 30-day avg of 155,000 shares

** YTD stock has shed 21%

($1 = 86.8230 Indian rupees)

data.title: New Zealand Shares Rise Amid Wall Street Gains; Manufacturing Activity Rises for First Time in 23 Months
data.description:
New Zealand shares advanced Friday in lockstep with Wall Street as investors weighed the latest development in the US' crackdown on what it calls unfair trade practices by other nations.

The S&P/NZX 50 Index was up 0.6%, or 83.20 points, to close at 12,989.18.

The benchmark index has risen 0.9% over the last five trading days.

Nearly all sectors recorded an increase Friday, with electronic technology and non-energy minerals taking the lead. Commercial services shares slipped 0.2%.

Shares in Asia were mixed, with Hong Kong's Hang Seng rallying 2.2%, the Shanghai SSE advancing 0.3%, and Japan's Nikkei 225 down 0.5%.

Overnight, the Nasdaq Composite increased 1.5%, the S&P 500 rose 1%, and the Dow Jones Industrial Average was up 0.8%.

US President Donald Trump signed a memorandum ordering the development of a "Fair and Reciprocal Plan" to tackle "unfair trade practices."

In domestic news, the BusinessNZ Performance of Manufacturing Index rose to 51.4 in January from 46.2 in the prior month, marking its highest level since September 2022 and the first expansion in 23 months.

Food prices in New Zealand rose 1.9% month on month in January, a leap from the 0.1% uptick in December 2024 to mark the highest increase since July 2022, Stats NZ data showed Friday.

In corporate news, Manawa Energy said the High Court ordered the company to dispense with formal service the amended application for its scheme of arrangement with Contact Energy . Manawa's shares finished almost 2% lower.

SKY Network Television rose past 1% after it moved the expected completion of its satellite migration to April from May.

data.title: Eneos Holdings Says Listing of Subsidiary JX Advanced Metals Approved
data.description:
(04:11 GMT) *Eneos Holdings: Tokyo Stock Exchange Approves Listing of Subsidiary JX Advanced Metals
(04:15 GMT) *Eneos: To Sell 465.2M Shares of JX Advanced Metals
(04:18 GMT) *Eneos: Additional 69.8M JX Advanced Metals Shares May Be Offered
(04:36 GMT) Eneos Holdings Says Listing of Subsidiary JX Advanced Metals Approved

By Kosaku Narioka

Eneos Holdings said the listing of subsidiary JX Advanced Metals was approved by the Tokyo Stock Exchange on Friday.

The Japanese energy company said it would sell 465.2 million shares of the unit and that an additional 69.8 million shares might be offered in the initial public offering.

JX Advanced Metals said the listing is expected to take place on March 19 and that the offering price will be determined on March 10. For now, the company expects to offer shares at 862 yen, equivalent to $5.64 apiece, it said.

Write to Kosaku Narioka at kosaku.narioka@wsj.com

data.title: AD Ports Reports Higher Preliminary FY24 Net Profit, Revenue
data.description:
Abu Dhabi Ports Co. (ADX:ADPORTS), d/b/a AD Ports, said Friday that its full-year 2024 preliminary net profit and revenue rose year over year.

Net profit attributable to owners of the company for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 1.33 billion Emirati dirhams, compared with 1.07 billion dirhams a year ago. EPS moved to 0.26 dirham from 0.21 dirham.

The Abu Dhabi-listed logistics and industry group's revenue was 17.29 billion dirhams, compared with 11.68 billion dirhams earlier.

data.title: Swiss National Bank's Tschudin says policy choices include FX intervention, negative rates
data.description:
The Swiss National Bank (SNB) remains committed to price stability, with inflation targeted between 0% and 2%, despite recent leadership changes, according to board member Petra Tschudin. She emphasized that the SNB's tools and policies remain unchanged under new Chairman Martin Schlegel, who succeeded Thomas Jordan last year.

Tschudin highlighted that while inflation may temporarily fall outside the target range, the medium-term outlook is what matters most. She reaffirmed that the SNB’s policy toolkit includes foreign exchange interventions and, if necessary, negative interest rates—a tool the bank used from 2014 to 2022. Negative rates could help manage interest rate differentials and prevent excessive appreciation of the Swiss franc, which could lower inflation and harm exporters.

Markets anticipate a potential rate cut at the SNB’s March 18 meeting following a drop in Swiss inflation to 0.4% in January. Tschudin indicated that the bank would consider reintroducing negative rates if needed, echoing recent comments from Chairman Schlegel, though both expressed a preference to avoid such a move unless necessary.

Members of the SNB policy board often visit ForexLive global headquarters for a quick chat.

Only kidding. Have a good weekend when it rocks around for you!

data.title: India bond yields dip; traders eye cues from $4.5 billion debt sale
data.description:
Indian government bond yields inched lower on Friday as U.S. yields were off their recent highs, even as major focus remained on the fresh debt supply through the weekly auction.

The benchmark 10-year yield (IN067934G=CC) was at 6.7000%, as of 10:00 a.m. IST, compared with its previous close of 6.7103%.

The 10-year U.S. yield eased over 10 basis points overnight, after certain components in the Producer Price Index (PPI) for January pointed to lower inflation.

New Delhi is set to raise 390 billion rupees ($4.49 billion) through a sale of bonds, including 220 billion rupees of the benchmark security.

"Demand for the benchmark note at current levels would be an interesting cue for movement in the remaining part of the month," a trader with a state-run bank said.

The Reserve Bank of India bought bonds worth 400 billion rupees on Thursday, with nearly 50% consisting of debt with a duration of more than 10 years.

However, the cutoff yields at which the offers were accepted were higher than market estimates, which weakened sentiment as it indicated that banks were desperate to clear their stock, traders said.

The RBI will conduct a 49-day repo for 750 billion rupees later in the day, followed by its last scheduled OMO purchase of bonds worth 200 billion rupees on February 20.

"Another positive trigger could come in the form of the RBI increasing the quantum of next week's OMO auction like they did for this week," the trader added.

The RBI has bought bonds worth 988.35 billion rupees through auctions and secondary purchases, infused 500 billion rupees through a 56-day repo and around 440 billion rupees via a dollar/rupee swap in the last one month.

As the mega liquidity infusion package nears its end, investors will also eye the central bank's next set of measures, as liquidity is set to tighten further into the financial year-end in March.

($1 = 86.8170 Indian rupees)

data.title: India stocks, rupee, swaps, call at 10:00 a.m. IST
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex was up 0.23% to 76,313, while the broader NSE Nifty 50 advanced 0.18% to 23,072, after U.S. President Donald Trump said the Indian Prime Minister offered to talk about lowering tariffs, easing some worries over reciprocal duties.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee rose 0.05% versus the U.S. dollar to 86.85 to dollar, tracking an uptick in its Asian peers on relief that U.S. President Donald Trump reciprocal tariff's plan will not be implemented till later.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN067934G=CC) was quoted at 100.62 rupees, with the yield down 1 bp at 6.7004% as traders await debt supply through weekly auction.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was not yet traded, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) dropped 4 bps to 6.10%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 6.45%, and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 6.34%

data.title: CelcomDigi's 2025 Earnings Outlook Could Be Promising — Market Talk
data.description: CelcomDigi's 2025 earnings outlook appears promising as depreciation costs ease post-merger and service revenue is guided for low single-digit growth versus a 0.6% decline in 2024, Kenanga IB analyst Kylie Chan Sze Zan says in a note. Post-merger synergies are expected to generate annual cost savings of MYR700 million-MYR800 million by 2027, she notes. However, 5G access fees to Digital Nasional, Malaysia's sole 5G service provider, may rise in 2025 under new conditions coming into effect, though the exact amount remains uncertain, she says. With network integration progressing well, CelcomDigi's outlook remains positive, she adds. Kenanga raises CelcomDigi's target price to MYR4.10 from MYR3.82, while maintaining an outperform rating on the stock. Shares are 7.2% lower at MYR3.59.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Bupa Arabia Secures Qualification Renewal for Saudi Health Branch
data.description:
Bupa Arabia for Cooperative Insurance (SASE:8210) secured qualification renewal from the Insurance Authority for its health insurance branch in Saudi Arabia.

The renewal is valid for one year, starting from Feb. 16, 2025, to Feb. 15, 2026, the insurance company said Thursday.

data.title: CGN Power Obtains Shenzhen Bourse Approval for Convertible Bond Issue
data.description:
Power supplier CGN Power secured approval from the Shenzhen Stock Exchange for the issue of A-share convertible bonds to unspecified entities.

The issuance is still subject to approval and registration by the China Securities Regulatory Commission, according to a Friday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

data.title: Market Chatter: Activist Investor Elliott Calls for Large Divestments at BP
data.description:
Elliott Management is urging BP (BP.L) to boost its performance and make certain divestments after building up a 5% stake in the oil major, Reuters reported on Thursday, citing a source familiar with the matter.

The activist investor is pushing for the sale of some assets in the low-carbon sector, among other businesses, the source said, adding that Elliott is not seeking higher oil and gas investments or a merger, and is supportive of BP's independent strategy.

BP did not immediately respond to MT Newswires' request for comment.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Australian Mines Defines Areas for Exploration From Drilling Results at Brazil Project; Shares Rise 10%
data.description:
Australian Mines reported assay drilling results from its Jequie rare earth element project in Brazil and defined two areas for follow-up exploration to expand the footprint of known total rare earth oxide enrichment, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

The firm completed an auger drilling program, comprising 72 shallow auger drill holes, totaling 500.6 meters over two priority rare earth element-in-soil targets.

The results included a "significant" intercept of 15 meters at 1,720 parts-per-million (ppm) grade of total rare earth oxides, including 3 meters at 3,055 ppm grade of total rare earth oxides. The company said 69% of the assays returned total rare earth oxide values greater than 400 ppm.

The firm's shares rose 10% in recent trading on Friday.

data.title: Pulse Seismic Declares Special Dividend of C$0.20 >PSD.T
South Korea's ICT Exports Decline 0.4% in January
data.description:
South Korea's exports of information and communications technology goods fell 0.4% to $16.3 billion in January from a year earlier, the Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy said Thursday.

Imports of ICT goods stood at $13.5 billion, which led to a trade balance surplus of $2.8 billion in the month, the release said.

Semiconductor exports rose 7.7% year-on-year to $10.1 billion in January, driven by AI demand and a 17.2% increase in memory chips to $6.2 billion. However, outbound shipments of system chips declined 4.3% to $3.5 billion.

Computer and peripheral exports climbed 10% to $0.9 billion, with solid-state drives up 27.1% to $600 million, marking 13 months of growth, according to the news release.

data.title: Santander Refutes Report of Potential Sale of UK Arm
data.description:
Banco Santander said its UK business is not for sale, Reuters reported Thursday, citing a spokesperson for the Spanish lender.

The bank made the statement after London's Financial Times reported the same day that it had early-stage talks with NatWest Group in 2024 regarding the potential divestment of its retail business in the UK. The FT report cited people familiar with the matter.

data.title: Eneos aims to raise as much as $3 bln via metals unit IPO
data.description:
JX Advanced Metals (JXAM), a wholly owned metals unit of Japan's Eneos Holdings , won approval to list its shares in a $3 billion share offering, a regulatory filing showed on Friday, in what would be the nation's biggest IPO in seven years.

JXAM has set a tentative offering price of 862 yen ($5.64) per share ahead of its listing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange on March 19.

The final pricing is set for March 10.

Eneos, Japan's largest oil refiner, plans to sell 465.2 million shares of JXAM with an overallotment option of up to 69.8 million shares. The share sale would help Eneos raise as much as 461 billion yen ($3.01 billion), according to Reuters' calculations.

($1 = 152.8600 yen)

data.title: Malaysia Q4 Foreign Direct Investment Largest in A Year
data.description:
Malaysia's foreign direct investment (FDI) increased to MYR 18.38 billion in Q4 of 2024 from MYR 14.52 billion in Q3, marking the largest amount since Q4 of 2023.

Most of the FDI flowed into the services sector, predominantly in the information & communication sub-sector and Financial activities.

This was followed by manufacturing, mining & quarrying, and agriculture sectors.

The main sources of FDI were the United States of America, Singapore, and Hong Kong.

data.title: United Breweries' Consolidated Profit Declines in Fiscal Q3
data.description:
United Breweries reported a sharp decline in its consolidated attributable profit to 384.0 million Indian rupees in the fiscal third quarter ended Dec. 31, 2024, against 853.4 million rupees a year ago.

Earnings per share contracted to 1.45 rupees compared with the year-ago figure of 3.23 rupees, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian bourses by the beermaker.

Revenue from operations in fiscal Q3, however, increased to 44.3 billion rupees from 41.6 billion rupees a year earlier.

Shares of the company were up over 2% in recent trade.
